author: Jeff Thieleke
content:
“Software startups in particular often mistakenly attribute their initial success to technology when, in reality, it’s because of people and timing.”

+100! (and don’t forget a healthy amount of luck and sloppy competition)

Another facet is that each department in a successful company often thinks that they are the main reason for the success, and therefore they need to double down on themselves. This can mean growing the sales team too quickly then struggling to find new products and use cases to sell. Or it can mean the development team loses focus on the customer and ends up chasing the latest technology.
